Drought and Flood: NOLA CAREER Project

Connor Whalen

2025-02-18

Introduction

This file contains all code pertaining to the CAREER project in NOLA’s drought and flood analysis.

Setup

Install packages.

Load packages.

Load full datasets (parasites collected and NOAA SPI precipitation data) and prep data for analysis

Drought / Flood analysis and data prepping

Controls

Find the average date of a hurricane, so that we can identify when the average hurricane starts and stops and how long it lasts – this will be used to assign a period of time to our hurricane controls. [This code was used to identify these information, but no longer needs to be run for the analysis to work]. Then, pick out the years in which there was no hurricane, so that we can identify which years to assign as control in our hurricane analysis. [These are dates of average drought as it pertains to the initial analysis plan (the one that mirrors the hurricane analysis plan) – not currently in use.]

Add actual drought/flood timeline parameters for 6 month (6 month meaning a buffer period of 6 months before a drought/flood [denoted as pre] and 6 months after a drought/flood [denoted post]). These dates for drought/flood occurrence are taken from the NOAA flooding website and the CAREER proposal from the Wood Lab, and represent all drought/flood events that occurred near the Pearl River in Louisiana across the sampling period. [This is the old/original analysis the same as hurricanes, but we are going to use the alternate analysis method of the matched SPI data instead – not currently in use.]

Visualization and checking of data frame.

Plots

Create color palette and assign faceting labels.

Mutate the drought/flood dataset so that the total sum of each type of parasite can be plotted:

Various plots.

Models

Drought/Flood count (abundance)

summary(drought_flood_count <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance ~ event*timing*psite_type + (total_length_mm|fish_sp_x/parasite_taxonomic_group) + (1|ci/catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_y/psite_spp_y), 
                          data = drought_flood_df, family = truncated_nbinom1, ziformula = ~ 1))
##  Family: truncated_nbinom1  ( log )
## Formula:          
## psite_abundance ~ event * timing * psite_type + (total_length_mm |  
##     fish_sp_x/parasite_taxonomic_group) + (1 | ci/catalog_number) +  
##     (1 | year_collected/season) + (1 | fish_sp_y/psite_spp_y)
## Zero inflation:                   ~1
## Data: drought_flood_df
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##       NA       NA       NA       NA     9192 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups                             Name            Variance  Std.Dev. Corr  
##  parasite_taxonomic_group:fish_sp_x (Intercept)     8.330e-02 0.28861        
##                                     total_length_mm 5.698e-04 0.02387  -0.91 
##  fish_sp_x                          (Intercept)     4.501e-02 0.21215        
##                                     total_length_mm 1.897e-04 0.01377  -0.87 
##  catalog_number:ci                  (Intercept)     4.606e-01 0.67867        
##  ci                                 (Intercept)     2.267e-03 0.04762        
##  season:year_collected              (Intercept)     4.451e-03 0.06672        
##  year_collected                     (Intercept)     2.351e-02 0.15332        
##  psite_spp_y:fish_sp_y              (Intercept)     3.162e+01 5.62292        
##  fish_sp_y                          (Intercept)     5.659e-02 0.23788        
## Number of obs: 9264, groups:  
## parasite_taxonomic_group:fish_sp_x, 39; fish_sp_x, 7; catalog_number:ci, 129; ci, 2; season:year_collected, 51; year_collected, 29; psite_spp_y:fish_sp_y, 148; fish_sp_y, 7
## 
## Dispersion parameter for truncated_nbinom1 family (): 17.3 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                                          Estimate Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                              -4.62834        NaN
## eventdrought                                             -1.32531        NaN

Create diagnostics.

# diagnostics
#Evaluate residuals
s = simulateResiduals(fittedModel = drought_flood_count, n = 250)
plot(s)

# other possible diagnostics
#df <- ggpredict(model, terms = "column[n=100]")
